ocrNote: Manual lineup; seed rebuilt from client/src/data/bassCanyon2026Lineup.js (no OCR). Deduped Excision rows; bios tightened (manual patch).

artists.name: ATLiens
artists.bio: Masked bass duo: alien, heavy, and high-energy for big-stage moments.

artists.name: Barely Alive
artists.bio: Dubstep duo with aggressive sound design and festival-ready anthems.

artists.name: Bear Grillz
artists.bio: Rowdy, personality-forward dubstep with classic festival punch.

artists.name: Big Gigantic
artists.bio: Sax + electronics: live-instrument energy over big groove drops.

artists.name: Borgore
artists.bio: Scene OG energy — dubstep and electro with a chaotic sense of fun.

artists.name: Calcium
artists.bio: Riddim-forward producer — sharp drums and unapologetically heavy low end.

artists.name: Canabliss
artists.bio: Colorful, bass-forward project built for high-motion festival sets.

artists.name: Crankdat
artists.bio: Trap-leaning festival energy: crisp drums, huge hooks, big-room bounce.

artists.name: Culture Shock
artists.bio: UK DnB precision — clean mixes and rolling stage presence.

artists.name: Dion Timmer
artists.bio: Melodic-leaning dubstep with a playful, high-detail production style.

artists.name: Dirtyphonics
artists.bio: French team blending DnB, dubstep, and festival electro in one set.

artists.name: EAZYBAKED
artists.bio: Southern weird-bass: smoked-out low end, trippy texture, and heavy swings.

artists.name: Emorfik
artists.bio: Riddim-leaning producer with sharp percussion and a heavier modern edge.

artists.name: Excision
artists.bio: Bass Canyon host and dubstep centerpiece—multiple sets on the bill, including a dedicated Detox set and a Slander B2B (per official poster).

artists.name: Slander
artists.bio: Melodic bass duo: emotional builds and huge singalong festival drops.

artists.name: FuntCase
artists.bio: UK scene staple — grimy, aggressive, and unapologetically rowdy energy.

artists.name: Ganja White Night
artists.bio: Wobbly, character-filled dub with a worldbuilding vibe end-to-end.

artists.name: Ghastly
artists.bio: Electro-leaning bass with a sleek, high-energy modern festival feel.

artists.name: Ghengar
artists.bio: Riddim-forward producer — sharp, stompy, and made for the rail.

artists.name: Heyz
artists.bio: Undercard pick with a bass-forward, club-to-stage punch.

artists.name: Illenium
artists.bio: Arena-scale melodic dance music — big chords, big feelings, big drops.

artists.name: INZO
artists.bio: Left-field bass: weird, cinematic, and made for “what was that?!” moments.

artists.name: JKYL & HYDE
artists.bio: Bass and trap energy — high-motion sets built to move a big floor.

artists.name: Kill Safari
artists.bio: Hard-hitting bass/trap blend — great for big-room hype moments.

artists.name: Level Up
artists.bio: Riddim DJ energy — quick cuts, fast drums, and heavy low-end focus.

artists.name: Mersiv
artists.bio: Liquid and crunch colliding — scenic, vibey, and still filthy when it counts.

artists.name: Muzz
artists.bio: DnB producer with a stadium-sized melodic streak and big breakdowns.

artists.name: NGHTMRE
artists.bio: Trap meets bass: festival-sized hooks, drops, and crowd chants on repeat.

artists.name: Nikita, The Wicked
artists.bio: Villain-coded bass — dark, dramatic, and theatrical in the best way.

artists.name: Pegboard Nerds
artists.bio: Norwegian duo: electro, dubstep, and melodic energy in a tight package.

artists.name: Ray Volpe
artists.bio: Festival-scale dubstep with laser-focused bangers and crowd-chant energy.

artists.name: Ravenscoon
artists.bio: Weird, playful bass; billed B2B with Jantsen on the poster—two flavors, one set.

artists.name: Jantsen
artists.bio: Groovy, heavy, personality-forward dubstep; B2B with Ravenscoon at this fest.

artists.name: Sigma with Armani Reign
artists.bio: Crossover DnB with Armani Reign in the mix — hooks and rollers built for a big field.

artists.name: Smith.
artists.bio: Dubstep selector with a crisp, high-energy style—built for a tight undercard set before the night peaks.

artists.name: Smoakland
artists.bio: West-coast lean with grimy bounce — dubstep for late-night head-nodding.

artists.name: Sullivan King
artists.bio: Metal + dubstep: scream-along energy and pit-friendly festival chaos.

artists.name: Svdden Death presents VOYD
artists.bio: The VOYD side of Svdden Death: dark, widescreen, brutally heavy.

artists.name: The Resistance
artists.bio: Collaborative or branded bass moment on the bill—treat it like a special 'show within the show' set.

artists.name: The Stoogs
artists.bio: Off-kilter bass and left-field energy—comedy-leaning name, serious weight when the subs hit.

artists.name: Virtual Riot
artists.bio: Sound-design masterclass — pretty melodies into aggressive bass payoffs.

artists.name: Whipped Cream
artists.bio: Trap, hip-hop, and bass crossover: vocal hooks, hard drums, and big-room energy.

artists.name: $J
artists.bio: Bass and experimental club textures—an early-day discovery slot for curious ears.

artists.name: All The Reason
artists.bio: Melodic bass and emotional build-ups—good for a golden-hour or pre-headliner mood shift.

artists.name: Austeria
artists.bio: Dark, cinematic low end—drama-first bass built for a packed tent or late-lane slot.

artists.name: Badklaat
artists.bio: UK riddim and dubstep: grimy, swaggering, and built for the rail.

artists.name: Brainrack
artists.bio: Gnarly, technical dubstep and riddim-leaning chaos—spiky, fast, rail-friendly.

artists.name: Wiley
artists.bio: Low-end–forward selector on this bill: rolling energy and room-filling bass without playing it safe.

artists.name: Capochino
artists.bio: Bounce-heavy bass and party-forward mixing—meant to move a floor fast.

artists.name: Crumb Pit
artists.bio: Gritty, crunchy dubstep with a “stop scrolling and get in the pit” feel.

artists.name: Dennett
artists.bio: Rising name on the undercard: tight, heavy, and worth catching early.

artists.name: Dodge & Fuski
artists.bio: UK-bred dubstep and electro: rowdy, dancefloor-proven, and built to sprint.

artists.name: Domina
artists.bio: Dark, assertive low end—moody and intense, made for a late set when the crowd wants danger.

artists.name: Dream Takers
artists.bio: Melodic and trap-influenced bass—hooky, vocal-friendly energy with a hard drop spine.

artists.name: Flozone
artists.bio: Textured dubstep: forward, weird, and built for a packed tent.

artists.name: Hekler
artists.bio: Brutish hybrid bass — crisp drums, sharp drops, and a rowdy feel.

artists.name: Hexxa
artists.bio: Tight, modern dubstep: sharp transients, heavy drops, and a fast-mixing pace.

artists.name: Hostage Situation
artists.bio: In-your-face bass—high tension, big drops, and a rowdy, confrontational edge.

artists.name: Ivory
artists.bio: Melodic bass textures — good for a sunset-adjacent, emotional set.

artists.name: Izadi
artists.bio: Primal, aggressive bass—short, punchy sets that land hard between bigger names.

artists.name: Justin Hawkes
artists.bio: UK DnB with festival polish — clean rollers and jump-up moments.

artists.name: KLO
artists.bio: Bass selector with a modern, undercard “wait, this is hard” feel.

artists.name: Liquid Smoak
artists.bio: Hazy, liquid-leaning low end in the Smoakland family lane—groovy, smoked-out, and vibey.

artists.name: Luci
artists.bio: Bass and electronic pop crossover—earworm hooks with enough sub to still feel at home here.

artists.name: Machaki
artists.bio: Fresh undercard: tight sets and heavy, modern bass color.

artists.name: Neotek
artists.bio: Future-leaning bass textures—late-night, neon, and a little cyber in the low end.

artists.name: Otsukare
artists.bio: Weird, playful bass—lighter weirdness to break up back-to-back heavy blocks.

artists.name: Paper Skies
artists.bio: Melodic, airy, and still weighty—floaty top lines with subs that still punch.

artists.name: PHRVA
artists.bio: Stage-forward energy with sharp, present mixes—meant to hold attention in a busy day.

artists.name: Pretty Sweet
artists.bio: Cute name, mean subs—bass with hooky moments and a sugary-meets-savage feel.

artists.name: Rossy
artists.bio: High-energy, club-born bass with a mainstage-ready bounce.

artists.name: Seth David
artists.bio: Groove-first DJ: bass and house textures that keep bodies moving.

artists.name: Siren
artists.bio: Cinematic, dramatic bass—tension-and-release energy that leans story-driven.

artists.name: Sumthin Sumthin
artists.bio: Funky, textured bass and future beats—groove-first, weird-second, in the best way.

artists.name: Super Future
artists.bio: Melodic future bass and emotional peaks—great for a softer lane between heavier blocks.

artists.name: Tripp St.
artists.bio: High-impact trap and bass hybrid—punchy drums, vocal chops, and festival-sized payoffs.

artists.name: Twopercent
artists.bio: Quirky undercard with internet-era bass humor and actual weight behind it.

artists.name: TYNAN
artists.bio: Trap-dubstep hybrid: precise drums, festival-sized drops, and clean mixes.

artists.name: Usaybflow
artists.bio: Future-leaning trap and bass: catchy leads, big drums, and drops sized for a daytime field.

artists.name: Vaski
artists.bio: Melodic, grittier bass edges — a nice in-between of pretty and filthy.

artists.name: Versa
artists.bio: Bass and experimental electronic textures—moody builds with a low-end that keeps evolving.

artists.name: Yosuf
artists.bio: Bright, vocal-led bass and trap crossover—anthem-y moments with a heavy finish.

artists.name: Z3LLA
artists.bio: Riddim-leaning cuts—fast, spiky, and engineered for the rail when the night turns mean.
